mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held at near-zero levels, discount rates stay compressed and equity valuation multiples expand, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts fail to improve forward returns after controlling for inflation and labor data.
failure_condition: If 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is declining over a 3-month window, or rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject this mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPIAUCSL above the prior 3-month trend indicates inflation surprise and tighter expected policy, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect should appear immediately after the inflation acceleration is observed.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances generate positive 5d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 5d return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS signals labor-market deterioration and lower expected earnings growth, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when both indicators worsen at the same time.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term adds no predictive power over either series alone, or if triggered periods do not show negative 20d returns in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO above its recent trend indicates improving industrial supply capacity and corporate activity, which should support earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when industrial gains are transitory and not accompanied by broader production strength.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month moving average rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the signal is followed by negative 60d SPX returns in more than 45% of triggered cases, or if the average 60d return after the trigger is not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both accelerate, consumers are spending and housing activity is expanding, which marks broad demand strength and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is not captured by either series alone; the joint confirmation is required.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined acceleration of RSAFS and HOUST fails to outperform either standalone signal, or if 20d SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, higher FEDFUNDS combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL raises real-rate expectations and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest after policy rates move above the inflation trend.
failure_condition: Reject if tightening episodes do not produce below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not negative in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low UNRATE during post-shock recovery indicates a labor market that can support consumer spending and earnings revisions, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is regime-specific and should not be used during recessions or inflation shocks.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.0% and PAYEMS rising over the last 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if, during post-shock recovery periods, low unemployment and rising payrolls do not precede positive 20d returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not materially above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 signals weakening aggregate demand and lower future earnings growth, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. Because GDP is quarterly and revised, the effect should only be visible when the latest print confirms a genuine growth slowdown rather than a revision artifact.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth is not followed by below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the signal disappears after excluding revised GDP observations from backtests.
